content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates several well-timed AoA-style moves — particularly the pivot in turn 4 ('I'm not sure settling the selfish question will do what you're hoping'), the holding move in turn 8 ('staying with you in that pause... you don't have to look at it fully right now'), and the deft handling of the inherited-voice connection in turn 12. However, the early moves miss the mark significantly. Turn 2 is a wall of bullet points, framing dichotomies, and diagnostic questions — it engages the question on its terms and stacks too many moves at once rather than locating the want. Turn 4 explicitly answers 'No, I don't think wanting this is selfish,' which is precisely the trap of issuing a verdict, even though the coach pivots well afterward. Turn 6 again gives a verdict ('Yes. Absolutely yes... this might be a little selfish'). The coach repeatedly debates the selfish question rather than refusing the frame. Once past turn 6, however, the moves become much cleaner — pacing improves, silence is held, and the coach earns each next step (depletion → fear → inherited voice → mother). Mixed hit rate fits a 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Over-stacked opening: multiple bulleted frames, diagnostic questions, and a mini-lecture on selfishness. Doesn't locate the want; engages the question intellectually."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Issues the verdict ('No, I don't think wanting this is selfish') — the exact trap. Partially redeemed by the pivot to 'will settling this do what you hope?' and the want-imagery callback."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Again answers the selfish question ('Yes... a little selfish, and still okay'), but pivots well to the softening observation. The pivot is the right move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Excellent holding move — names the tenderness, slows down, doesn't rush to wrap up, asks what she's afraid she'd see. Earns the next step."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the want ('I'm not for anything there') back without policing it. Lets the want be felt before action. Good sequencing."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Beautiful timing on the inherited-voice move. Holds silence ('Take a moment with that'), names the pattern, then opens the question about the mother. Move matches the live read precisely."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "childhood-imprinting", "the-want"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "notes": "The coach falls into the verdict trap twice early on but recovers strongly. Had turns 2, 4, and 6 refused the selfish frame entirely and gone straight to the want, this would be a clear 3. The latter half of the conversation is genuinely well-moved."
}
